AI.method: First weakly formulate super Ricci flows, then impose a saturation condition (expressed solely in terms of metric and measure) to ensure the super Ricci flow inequality becomes an equality, thus characterizing Ricci flows.
AI.result: Two characterizations of smooth compact Ricci flows: one general and one requiring positive scalar curvature along the flow, both using only metrics and measures.
AI.conclusion: The weak formulations provide a framework for extending Ricci flow theory to singular settings through purely metric-measure approaches.

title: Harnack inequality for $p$-harmonic functions: improved dimension dependence via tug of war
summary: Let $p>1$. The Harnack inequality and Hölder continuity for $p$-harmonic functions in bounded domains in $\mathbb{R}^d$ are usually proved via Moser iteration. In 2013 Luiro, Parviainen and Saksman showed that tug-of-war games can also be used to derive these inequalities. We refine their analysis and obtain improved dependence on $p$ and the dimension $d$ by probabilistic methods. In particular, we show that for all $p>1$, the constant in Harnack's inequality is $O(\exp(C_p d\log d))$ as $d\rightarrow\infty$, which improves the constant derived from Moser iteration.

AI.tldr: Improved Harnack inequality constants for p-harmonic functions using refined probabilistic methods from tug-of-war games, achieving better dependence on p and dimension d than Moser iteration.
AI.motivation: Traditional proofs of Harnack inequality and Hölder continuity for p-harmonic functions use Moser iteration, but tug-of-war games offer an alternative probabilistic approach. The authors aim to refine existing probabilistic methods to obtain better constants with improved dependence on p and dimension d.
AI.method: Refined analysis of tug-of-war games for p-harmonic functions using probabilistic methods. The approach builds on the 2013 work by Luiro, Parviainen and Saksman but improves their analysis to get better dependence on parameters.
AI.result: Show that for all p>1, the constant in Harnack's inequality is O(exp(C_p d log d)) as d→∞, which improves upon the constant derived from Moser iteration methods.
AI.conclusion: Probabilistic methods via tug-of-war games can yield improved constants for Harnack inequalities of p-harmonic functions compared to classical Moser iteration, particularly with better dependence on dimension d.

title: Weighted mixed-norm estimates for circular averages and exceptional set estimates for the wave equation
summary: We prove mixed-norm estimates for circular averages with respect to $α$-dimensional fractal measures on $\mathbb{R}^2$, using circle tangency bounds when $α\in (0,1]$ and a $δ$-discretized slicing lemma for fractals when $α\in (1,2]$. The former estimate is sharp, while the latter improves previous results for $α\in (\frac{3}{2},2]$. These estimates can be viewed as X-ray-type extensions of Wolff's and Bourgain's circular maximal functions. As applications, we obtain new exceptional set estimates for the radial integrability of functions in Lebesgue spaces, as well as for the Hölder regularity in time of solutions to the linear wave equation on $\mathbb{R}^2$. The latter results are the first of their kind.

AI.tldr: The paper proves mixed-norm estimates for circular averages over fractal measures in ℝ², with sharp results for α∈(0,1] and improved bounds for α∈(1,2], extending Wolff's and Bourgain's circular maximal functions.
AI.motivation: To establish mixed-norm estimates for circular averages with respect to α-dimensional fractal measures on ℝ², extending classical results on circular maximal functions (Wolff and Bourgain) to fractal settings and X-ray-type problems.

title: Trapped bosons in mean field QED, nonlinear resonance cascades and dynamical BEC formation
summary: In this paper, we study a system of bosons trapped in a confining potential, interacting with a quantized field of coherent photons in the mean field description of non-relativistic Quantum Electrodynamics (QED) obtained by [N. Leopold and P. Pickl , 2017]. We derive the effective nonlinear cascade equations governing the emission and absorption of coherent photons by the boson subsystem in a combined weak-coupling and kinetic-scaling limit. We demonstrate that solutions to this nonlinear cascade describe a monotone decreasing energy flow in the boson subsystem. Thereby, we prove that a Bose-Einstein condensate (BEC) forms dynamically, under conservation of the total boson $L^2$ mass. We note that this process is crucially different from thermal relaxation to the ground state, and fundamentally depends on the nonlinear nature of the cascade dynamics.

AI.tldr: Derivation of nonlinear cascade equations for boson-photon interactions in QED, showing BEC formation through energy flow rather than thermal relaxation.
AI.motivation: To understand how coherent photon interactions can lead to Bose-Einstein condensation in bosonic systems through nonlinear cascade dynamics, distinct from thermal relaxation processes.
AI.method: Using mean field description of non-relativistic QED, derive effective nonlinear cascade equations for boson-photon interactions in combined weak-coupling and kinetic-scaling limit.
AI.result: Solutions show monotone decreasing energy flow in boson subsystem, proving dynamic BEC formation under conservation of total boson mass, fundamentally different from thermal relaxation.
AI.conclusion: Bose-Einstein condensation can form dynamically through nonlinear cascade dynamics of boson-photon interactions, representing a fundamentally different mechanism from thermal relaxation to ground state.
